item.description: The United States and Iran once again exchanged fire, sending oil prices rising and casting new doubt on peace talks. Iranian media report that Iran's forces first fired warning shots as American ships tried to pass through the Strait of Hormuz without authorization and that the U.S. military responded with strikes. An American official described the strikes as self-defense. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: US-Iran Peace Talks in Doubt After Second Clash in 3 Days
media:content.media:description: The United States and Iran once again exchanged fire, sending oil prices rising and casting new doubt on peace talks. Iranian media report that Iran's forces first fired warning shots as American ships tried to pass through the Strait of Hormuz without authorization and that the U.S. military responded with strikes. An American official described the strikes as self-defense. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

item.title: US-Iran Peace Talks in Doubt After Second Clash in 3 Days

item.dc:creator: Gabe Gutierrez
Ryan J. Reilly
Kyla Guilfoil
Megan Lebowitz
item.description: The Justice Department has opened a criminal investigation involving E. Jean Carroll’s lawsuits over her sexual abuse allegations against President Donald Trump, according to two sources familiar with the matter

media:content.media:text: E. Jean Carroll
media:content.media:description: The DOJ opened up an investigation related to E. Jean Carroll, who sued President Donald Trump for sexual abuse and defamation.

item.title: DOJ opens criminal probe involving E. Jean Carroll testimony in Trump sexual abuse lawsuit

item.dc:creator: Peter Nicholas
item.description: 13 farmers across Iowa said they’re struggling economically, but 11 voted for Trump in the past and still largely back him, hoping “he follows through” on his promises.

media:content.media:text: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer, at his home in Galt, Iowa.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Stu Swanson’s Hagie crop sprayer.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Newly planted corn grows in a field on Stu Swanson’s farm in Galt, Iowa.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: The Gold-Eagle Cooperative feed mill and grain facility, where Stu Swanson sells corn from his farm, is visible from his property in Galt.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Image: President Trump Speaks To Farmers At The White House
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Mark Mueller.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Steve Rehder and Corey, Doug and Don Winterfeld.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Jason Orr
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Stu Swanson's farm in Galt, Iowa.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Travis, Arda and Loren Van Regenmorter on their family farm in Sioux Center, Iowa.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: A photo in Stu Swanson’s home of his parents, Florine and Ron Swanson, who farmed the same land.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

item.title: Farmers in Iowa are struggling in Trump’s economy, but many say they still support him

item.dc:creator: Ramon Taylor
Marina Kopf
Maggie Vespa
item.description: Several weeks after NBC News reached out to Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield about the denial, the insurer changed its policy to include coverage of deep brain stimulation for certain children.

media:content.media:text: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Cambrie Sharp sits on a trampoline, a dog sits on the lawn in front of her
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: “The medications that typically work for kids don’t work for her," Brandi Sharp said. "They cause a lot of rebounds."
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Two hands are placed on top of denial letters
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Brandi Sharp stands underneath a budding tree outside her home
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Cambrie's doctors say deep brain stimulation could help reduce the frequency of her seizures.
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Cambrie Sharp lays on a trampoline
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

item.title: When drug after drug failed, a teen hoped brain surgery could fix her daily seizures. Insurance denied it.

item.description: An albino buffalo has been nicknamed “Donald Trump” for its tuft of pale hair, a feature many say mirrors the president’s signature hairstyle. Due to its popularity, the government spared the buffalo from the Eid al-Adha sacrifice and instead moved it to the Bangladesh National Zoo.

media:content.media:title: Sacrificial albino buffalo spared after Trump resemblance
media:content.media:description: An albino buffalo has been nicknamed “Donald Trump” for its tuft of pale hair, a feature many say mirrors the president’s signature hairstyle. Due to its popularity, the government spared the buffalo from the Eid al-Adha sacrifice and instead moved it to the Bangladesh National Zoo.

item.title: Sacrificial albino buffalo spared after Trump resemblance

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Soccer fans headed to Houston for the World Cup this summer can expect an out-of-this-world experience as the city combines its connection to the stars while roping in some of the best of what the city has to offer by keeping two feet on the ground.

media:content.media:text: The exterior of NRG Stadium with World Cup signage
media:content.media:description: NRG Stadium, which will be called Houston Stadium during the World Cup, on Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: An interior view of NRG Stadium, the astroturf is being sprayed with a pink substance
media:content.media:description: NRG Stadium, which will be called Houston Stadium during the World Cup, on Wednesday.

item.title: Houston planning out-of-this-world experience for World Cup fans this summer

item.description: New video captures the devastation inside the Nippon Paper Industries plant in Washington, moments after the implosion of a massive chemical tank. Authorities say two people were killed and at least nine are still missing. The cause of the disaster is still unknown. NBC’s Morgan Chesky reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: New Video Shows Devastation After Chemical Plant Implosion
media:content.media:description: New video captures the devastation inside the Nippon Paper Industries plant in Washington, moments after the implosion of a massive chemical tank. Authorities say two people were killed and at least nine are still missing. The cause of the disaster is still unknown. NBC’s Morgan Chesky reports for TODAY.

item.title: New Video Shows Devastation After Chemical Plant Implosion

item.dc:creator: NBC News
item.description: Today is a decision day at the Supreme Court, which has yet to issue rulings on consequential cases like Trump v. Barbara on birthright citizenship or Trump v. Lisa Cook, the Federal Reserve official.

media:content.media:text: NBC News's Laura Jarrett Answers Your Questions
media:content.media:description: Laura Jarrett.

item.title: Laura Jarrett answers your questions today about the Supreme Court and more

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
Adam Reiss
item.description: CBS News editor Bari Weiss a new executive producer at “60 Minutes,” the network’s flagship newsmagazine: Nick Bilton, a former New York Times tech columnist.

media:content.media:text: Nick Bilton.
media:content.media:description: Nick Bilton in Beverly Hills, Calif., in 2017.

media:content.media:text: Bari Weiss.
media:content.media:description: Nick Bilton in Beverly Hills, Calif., in 2017.

item.title: CBS News hires technology journalist Nick Bilton to run ‘60 Minutes’

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Grammy-winning artist Olivia Rodrigo is speaking out against backlash over a babydoll dress she wore while debuting her single “Drop Dead.”During a concert at Teatre Grec in Barcelona, Spain, earlier this month for Spotify’s Billions Club Live series, the singer-songwriter performed in a small pink floral dress with puffed sleeves

media:content.media:text: Olivia Rodrigo.
media:content.media:description: Olivia Rodrigo on May 8 in Barcelona, Spain.

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo reacts to dress criticism, calls out ‘normalized pedophilia in our culture’

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: In a case that raised questions about racial bias in the criminal justice system, the Supreme Court threw out the murder conviction of a Black death row inmate in Mississippi.

media:content.media:text: Supreme Court Releases Opinions

item.title: Supreme Court sides with death-row inmate who alleged racial bias in jury selection

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer said that she does not plan to run for president in 2028, rebuffing assumptions that she would join a crowded Democratic primary field.

media:content.media:text: Secretary Blinken And U.S. Governors Speak At The 2023 SelectUSA Investment Summit In Maryland
media:content.media:description: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer said she is not going to be part of the 2028 presidential field.

item.title: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer says she won’t run for president in 2028

item.dc:creator: Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: CVS Caremark will resume covering the weight loss drug Zepbound this year after it removed it from its list of covered medications last year, drugmaker Eli Lilly said.

media:content.media:text: A closeup of a Zepbound injection cartridge held between someone's fingertips.
media:content.media:description: Eli Lilly's Zepbound.

item.title: CVS Caremark will cover Lilly’s weight loss drug Zepbound again after patient backlash

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
Cory Fernandez
Bethany Heitman
item.description: I found the best methods and products for cleaning glasses, including reading glasses, sunglasses and more. Optometrists recommend the best cleaning products.

media:content.media:text: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.
media:content.media:description: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.

media:content.media:text: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.
media:content.media:description: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.

item.title: This is how you should actually be cleaning your glasses

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: The Ninja Creami is one of the best ice cream makers, but are there better options? I tested it against KitchenAid, Cuisinart Fast Freeze and more to find out.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Cory Fernandez and Zoe Malin
media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Cory Fernandez and Zoe Malin
media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami on a granite table with the mixing paddle beside it.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: Cuisinart FastFreeze ice cream maker on a table.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: Light green KitchenAid Stand Mixer with ice cream maker attachment installed.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The GreenPan Frost sitting on a table with the lid beside it.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker on a table.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

item.title: I tested the Ninja Creami against five popular competitors, and the results shocked me

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best Home Depot Memorial Day sales on tools, appliances, and gardening supplies from Dewalt, Miracle-Gro and LG.

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: Podiatrists say that Birkenstock Arizona sandals are some of the most comfortable shoes on the market thanks to the built-in arch support. Right now, get them for 40 percent off.

media:content.media:text: Birkenstock Arizona sandals have a contoured footbed with built-in arch support for comfort.
media:content.media:description: Birkenstock Arizona sandals have a contoured footbed with built-in arch support for comfort.

media:content.media:text: Birkenstock Arizona sandals have a contoured footbed with built-in arch support for comfort.
media:content.media:description: Birkenstock Arizona sandals have a contoured footbed with built-in arch support for comfort.

item.title: Deal of the Day: Birkenstock Arizona sandals are 40% off

item.title: Supreme Court sides with death-row inmate who alleged racial bias in jury selection

item.dc:creator: Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said in an interview clip released Wednesday that she thought her husband, former President Joe Biden, was “having a stroke” during his critical June 2024 debate, which catalyzed the chain of events that eventually led him to drop out of the presidential election.“I don’t know what happened.

media:content.media:text: US-POLITICS-ECONOMY-MILKEN
media:content.media:description: Former U.S. First Lady Dr. Jill Biden speaks during the 29th annual Milken Institute Global Conference at the Beverly Hilton in Beverly Hills, Calif., on May 5, 2026.

item.title: Jill Biden says she thought Joe Biden was ‘having a stroke’ during 2024 debate

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
Matt Dixon
item.description: Ken Paxton has trailed Democratic state Rep. James Talarico in fundraising. Some Republicans say the party will have to spend millions to win the Senate race.

media:content.media:text: Ken Paxton.
media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton, Texas attorney general and Republican US Senate candidate, greets attendees during a Texas primary election night event in Dallas, Texas, US, on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. US Senator John Cornyn and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton are heading for a runoff in the Republican primary for the US Senate in Texas, according to projections by DDHQ. Photographer: Antranik Tavitian/Bloomberg via Getty Images

item.title: Republicans brace for money problems in Texas after Ken Paxton’s win
